topics: 🦶 כֵּיצַד הָרֶגֶל מוּעֶדֶת — the foot's normal damage
🧱 שור שמתחכך בכותל — rubbing case, kofer, and tam
🛣️ Two-road case: animal chooses the pebble path
💩 R' Elazar: producing dung and the מקלוט rule

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Foot, Pebbles, and Edge Cases of הֶעָדָאָה
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Chapter 2 opens by asking: how is the foot mu'ad? The Mishnah lists three sub-cases — kicking, ricocheting pebbles, and the secondary breakage when a vessel falls on another. The Yerushalmi probes each: R' Immi reframes the pebble case to the moment a person was holding the vessel, R' Abba bar Mamal introduces the two-road scenario, and R' Elazar lays down a sweeping rule — anything חוּץ לְגוּפָהּ doesn't distinguish between domains for the half-damage rule. The daf then takes up הֶעָדָאָה for things-outside-the-body, the wall-rubbing ox, and the dung case.
overview.hebrewSource: כָּל דָּבָר שֶׁהוּא חוּץ לְגוּפָהּ לֹא חִלְּקוּ בוֹ חֲכָמִים בֵּין בִּרְשׁוּת הַיָּחִיד בֵּין בִּרְשׁוּת הָרַבִּים לַחֲצִי כּוֹפֶר

steps.content: 1️⃣ Default: הַבְּהֵמָה מוּעֶדֶת לְהַלֵּךְ כְּדַרְכָּהּ וּלְשַׁבֵּר — full damage for normal walking-breakage.
3️⃣ Steps on a vessel and breaks it; pieces fall on a second vessel and break that.
4️⃣ For the first vessel: full damage. For the second: half damage.
5️⃣ The Mishnah is mapping different layers: direct foot-damage vs. derivative trajectories.

callout.label: Key distinction:
callout.text: Direct contact = full. Anything launched (pebbles, fragments) = half. The animal's body is the dividing line.

slides.title: 💬 The Pebble Case — Where Does Liability Apply?
slides.subtitle: Three voices reframe the half-damage rule.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: וְאֵין דַּרְכָּהּ לְהַתִּיז צְרוֹרוֹת? — pebbles are normal walking-byproducts. Why pay anything?

lines.speaker: RI
lines.text: R' Immi: this is a case where a person was holding the vessel. If it was lying in רשות הרבים — animal walking properly, vessel sitting properly — exempt.

lines.speaker: RB
lines.text: R' Abba bar Mamal: imagine two roads — one with grasses and pebbles, one without. If the animal chose the pebble road, even in רשות הרבים, the owner is exempt.

lines.speaker: RE
lines.text: R' Elazar lays down the principle: כָּל דָּבָר שֶׁהוּא חוּץ לְגוּפָהּ לֹא חִלְּקוּ בוֹ חֲכָמִים בֵּין רשות היחיד לרשות הרבים לחצי כופר.

callout.label: Yerushalmi style:
callout.text: Each amora reads the half-damage rule against a different fact pattern. R' Elazar then collapses the variations under one principle: outside-the-body damage doesn't distinguish domains.

slides.title: 🪨 Direct vs. Outside-the-Body Damage
slides.subtitle: The conceptual axis of the chapter.

boxes.title: 🪨 צרורות (pebbles)
items: Pebbles fly from under the foot

boxes.title: 🍶 Falling fragments
items: Vessel A breaks → pieces hit vessel B

callout.label: The axis:
callout.text: Once the harm comes from something outside the animal's body, the half-damage rule kicks in and the domain distinction collapses.

slides.title: 🧱 Wall-Rubbing Ox — Can Tam Have כופר?
slides.subtitle: R' Ze'ira and R' Illa argue about הֶעָדָאָה for outside-the-body.

nodes.content: Q: Does הֶעָדָאָה apply to דבר שהוא חוץ לגופה?

columns.label: R' Ze'ira
columns.content: מְקַבֶּלֶת — yes, it can be made mu'ad

nodes.content: Beraita: שׁוֹר שֶׁמִּתְחַכֵּךְ בַּכּוֹתֶל וְנָפַל עַל הָאָדָם וַהֲרָגוֹ — חַיָיב בַּכּוֹפֶר וּפָטוּר מִמִּיתָה
Reading: there is כופר for tam? Resolution — the ox was already mu'ad to topple walls; in this case no human was at the wall when it fell.

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: The wall-rubbing case becomes the test case: if הֶעָדָאָה works on something חוץ לגופה, the beraita slots in cleanly. If not, the Yerushalmi has to read it as a standing mu'ad.

slides.title: 💩 R' Elazar's Dung Case
slides.subtitle: When does an animal's byproduct yield full damage?

steps.content: 1️⃣ הִטִּילָה גְלָלִים — the animal produced dung and damaged a vessel.
3️⃣ Counter: should we require an owner to keep a מקלוט (receptacle) handy?
4️⃣ Distinction: בְּשֶׁאֵין דַּרְכָּהּ לְכֵן — when this isn't habitual, no receptacle needed.
5️⃣ אֲבָל בְּשֶׁדַּרְכָּהּ לָכֵן צָרִיךְ שֶׁיְּהֵא הַמִּקְלוֹט בְּיָדוֹ — habitual behavior requires precautions.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: Habit reshapes liability. Once the animal regularly produces dung in damaging ways, the owner's failure to bring a receptacle becomes the gateway to full damage.

summary.principle: Foot damage runs on a body/outside-the-body axis: direct contact pays full, anything launched pays half — and that axis dissolves the usual domain distinctions.

flowSteps.content:
Mishnah 2:1: how is foot mu'ad?
לְשַׁבֵּר בְּדֶרֶךְ הִילּוּכָהּ

Direct walking → full
Pebbles / falling fragments → half

columns.content:
🧱 R' Ze'ira / R' Illa
can outside-body become mu'ad?

flowSteps.content:
🎓 Key Principle:
The foot is mu'ad to break in its path — but anything outside its body sits in a different liability regime. Habit (R' Elazar's dung case) and הֶעָדָאָה (R' Ze'ira) extend the rules to edge cases.

quiz.q: What does the Mishnah pay for pebbles ricocheting from under the foot?
o: Half damage from the body only
quiz.e: הָיוּ צְרוֹרוֹת מְנַתְּזִין... מְשַׁלֵּם חֲצִי נֶזֶק — pebbles trigger half damage.

quiz.q: If an animal steps on a vessel and the broken pieces fall on a second vessel, what is the rule?
o: Full damage on the first, half on the second
quiz.e: עַל הָרִאשׁוֹן מְשַׁלֵּם נֶזֶק שָׁלֵם וְעַל הָאַחֲרוֹן מְשַׁלֵּם חֲצִי נֶזֶק — direct contact is full, derivative is half.

quiz.q: How does R' Abba bar Mamal explain pebble liability?
o: Two roads — one with pebbles, one without; if the animal chose the pebble road, even in רשות הרבים the owner is exempt
Liability depends on the vessel's owner
quiz.e: R' Abba bar Mamal's two-road case: הִנִּיחָה אֶת שֶׁאֵין בָּהּ עֲשָׂבִים וּצְרוֹרוֹת וְהִילְּכָה בְזוֹ שֶׁיֵּשׁ בָּהּ — exempt even in public domain.

quiz.q: What is R' Elazar's general principle about outside-the-body damage?
o: Half-damage applies regardless of domain — לֹא חִלְּקוּ בוֹ חֲכָמִים בֵּין רשות היחיד לרשות הרבים
quiz.e: Anything חוּץ לְגוּפָהּ bypasses the domain distinction for the half-damage rule.

quiz.q: Why does R' Elazar say producing dung yields full damage when not habitual, but habitual cases require a מקלוט?
o: Because dung damage is always outside the body
Because if the animal regularly does this, the owner must take precautions (carry a receptacle); otherwise it's an unforeseen full-damage case
Because dung is treated like keren
Because dung is exempt in רשות הרבים
quiz.e: בְּשֶׁאֵין דַּרְכָּהּ לְכֵן — no receptacle needed; אֲבָל בְּשֶׁדַּרְכָּהּ לָכֵן צָרִיךְ שֶׁיְּהֵא הַמִּקְלוֹט בְּיָדוֹ. Habit creates the duty of precaution.
